# llm-client

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 47
- **Lines of code**: 9,980
- **Languages**: TypeScript (42), JSON (3), Other (2)
- **Zones**: 4 (4 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 3acc839
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-05T03:01:52.263Z

## Architecture Zones

### LLM CLI & Config Utilities (`llm-cli-utilities`)

Supporting infrastructure for CLI interaction, authentication, project configuration, output formatting, and JSON handling consumed by the provider core.

- Files: 19
- Cohesion: 0.65, Coupling: 0.35
- Entry points: src/config.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-cli-utilities/context.md`

### LLM Provider Core (`llm-provider-core`)

Vendor-neutral LLM abstraction layer defining provider interfaces, client factory, session management, and token usage tracking.

- Files: 13
- Cohesion: 0.60, Coupling: 0.40
- Entry points: src/api-provider.ts, src/cli-provider.ts, src/codex-cli-provider.ts, src/create-client.ts, src/llm-client.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-provider-core/context.md`

### LLM Provider Unit Tests (`llm-provider-tests`)

Comprehensive unit test suite covering all public provider types, client factory, registry, session lifecycle, and token usage.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-provider-tests/context.md`

### Package Root Configuration (`package-root`)

Top-level build and dependency configuration for the llm-client package.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/package-root/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/types.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/config.ts` (6 imports)
- `src/create-client.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/provider-registry.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/token-usage.ts` (4 imports)
- `src/api-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/cli-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/codex-cli-provider.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/exec.ts` (3 imports)
- `src/auth.ts` (2 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 20 files)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (used by 3 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- A shared base types zone is missing: both llm-provider-core and llm-cli-utilities need the same fundamental types (provider config shapes, token structs, project dir records), causing core to import upward into utilities to get them. Introducing a zero-dependency types-only zone that both source zones import downward from would eliminate the cycle with no behavioral change. [critical]
- HTTP and subprocess provider strategies (api-provider vs cli-provider/codex-cli-provider) are co-located in the same zone with no sub-boundary — a consumer cannot depend on just the HTTP strategy without also pulling in subprocess execution code, and adding a new transport requires editing files adjacent to unrelated transports. [warning]

### Patterns

- The asymmetry between test coverage (core only) and source coupling (core ↔ utilities) means the package's test suite validates the intended architecture but not the actual architecture — a dedicated utility test zone would close this gap. [warning]
- The three source zones form a layered architecture (utilities → core ← tests) that is one refactor away from being a clean strict DAG — only 3 import edges need to be redirected or extracted into a shared types module to achieve full unidirectionality. [warning]

### Relationships

- No zone imports from package-root at runtime — configuration is consumed by external tooling only. This clean boundary means config changes cannot introduce runtime regressions in source zones. [info]

### Observations

- Bidirectional coupling: "llm-cli-utilities" ↔ "llm-provider-core" (23+3 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Contains 40% of project files (19/47) — may be too broad, consider splitting [warning] (llm-cli-utilities)
- 12 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (llm-provider-core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (llm-provider-tests)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (package-root)
- All zones use a single public entry point pattern consistent with the monorepo's src/public.ts convention, making the package boundary explicit and auditable. [info]
- Four zones for a 41-file package indicates appropriate granularity — the community detection has produced meaningful boundaries without over-fragmenting a small package. [info]
- The bidirectional dependency between src and src-2 (25 down, 3 up) is the primary architectural risk — eliminating the 3 upward imports would make the layering clean and unidirectional. [warning]

### Suggestions

- tests/unit/ contains files from 3 zones (llm-cli-utilities, llm-provider-core, llm-provider-tests) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Document the one-to-one test-to-module mapping convention used in llm-provider-tests in a PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md or test/README.md entry — this implicit convention is enforced only by social norms and will silently erode as the package grows. Codifying it prevents new contributors from breaking the mapping during refactors. [info]
- The 3 reverse imports (llm-provider-core → llm-cli-utilities) combined with zero utility test coverage and implicit session↔auth coupling form a compounding risk cluster: a single change to auth.ts or a utility config shape can silently break provider construction with no test signal at any layer. Treat these three issues as a single fix unit: extract shared types, add a utility test zone, and add a session-auth integration test simultaneously. [warning]
- Establish a file-naming prefix or directory grouping convention that maps visibly to the zone name — e.g. group files under src/config/, src/auth/, src/cli/ subdirectories. The current flat structure with a zone name that does not appear in any filename makes zone membership invisible to contributors and tooling. [info] (llm-cli-utilities)
- Separate help-format.ts and output.ts (presentation concerns) from auth.ts and exec.ts (security and subprocess concerns) with at minimum a directory-level boundary within the zone. Mixing UI formatting and credential handling in an undifferentiated file set means a security audit of auth.ts must also consider whether output.ts inadvertently logs credentials. [warning] (llm-cli-utilities)
- Audit llm-types.ts to determine whether it holds provider-scoped or package-wide types. If it contains types shared with llm-cli-utilities (project dir shapes, token structs, provider config interfaces), move those types to a new zero-dependency types module — this is likely the direct cause of the 3 reverse imports and fixing the placement fixes the cycle structurally. [warning] (llm-provider-core)
- Rename codex-cli-provider.ts to follow the transport-first naming pattern used by api-provider and cli-provider — e.g. codex-provider.ts or openai-cli-provider.ts depending on whether the file abstraction is vendor or transport. Mixing both in the filename breaks the zone's naming convention and will compound as more vendors are added. [info] (llm-provider-core)
- Add an integration test file (e.g. tests/integration/session-auth.test.ts) that exercises provider-session.ts with real or mock auth context from llm-cli-utilities — this would be the first test to span the provider-core/cli-utilities boundary and would catch regressions introduced by the 3 reverse import edges. [warning] (llm-provider-tests)
